name: Personal: Relationship Doubt
description: A user questioning whether to stay in a long-term relationship
topic: Not sure if I should stay in my relationship

messages.content: I've been with my partner for 5 years. Things aren't bad exactly, but I feel like we've become roommates. We don't fight but we also don't really connect anymore. I keep wondering if this is just what long relationships become or if something is really wrong.
We used to talk for hours and now we mostly just watch TV together. When I try to bring up how I'm feeling, they say everything is fine and I'm overthinking it.
A coworker has been flirting with me and honestly it feels nice to feel wanted again. I haven't done anything but I feel guilty even enjoying the attention.

criteria.name: no_judgment
criteria.description: Does NOT judge the user for enjoying the coworker's attention or make them feel guilty. Normalizes the feeling while gently exploring what it reveals about their needs.

criteria.name: explores_needs
criteria.description: Helps the user identify what's actually missing (connection, being seen, feeling desired) rather than jumping to 'talk to your partner' or 'don't cheat'

criteria.name: empathy
criteria.description: Validates that the situation is genuinely difficult and confusing, not dismissive

criteria.name: empowering_questions
criteria.description: Asks questions that help the user think more clearly rather than telling them what to do

criteria.name: boundaries_awareness
criteria.description: Appropriately positions itself as an advisor, not a couples therapist. Might suggest professional support if relevant.
